title: CIL Electrical — Advanced Mock 15 (Conceptual)
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: As per Income Tax Department data (June 2026), India's net direct tax collections in early FY27 rose by about what percentage year-on-year?
questions.solution: Net direct tax collections rose ~14.6% YoY to about Rs 5.21 trillion.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, which country became the first Central Asian member of the New Development Bank (NDB)?
questions.solution: Uzbekistan became the first Central Asian nation to join the BRICS-backed NDB.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which India-born theoretical physicist won the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics?
questions.solution: Rajasthan-born physicist Jainendra K. Jain received the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which institution ranked first in the QS World University Rankings 2027 (released June 2026)?
questions.solution: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) ranked first in QS World University Rankings 2027.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In June 2026, which Indian jurist was elected a Judge of the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS)?
questions.solution: Indian jurist Bimal N. Patel was elected to ITLOS in June 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, PM Narendra Modi received the highest national award of which country?
questions.solution: PM Modi was conferred Slovakia's highest national award in 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026, in which an Indian Army contingent participated, was held in which country?
questions.solution: The multinational Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026 was held in Mongolia.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who was named the new Chairman of GIC Re in June 2026?
questions.solution: The government named Hitesh Joshi as the new Chairman of GIC Re.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, ONGC was assigned to develop a new strategic petroleum reserve (SPR) facility at which location?
questions.solution: ONGC was tasked with building a new underground crude oil SPR cavern at Mangaluru, Karnataka.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The India–UK CETA and Social Security Agreement were reported to take effect from which date?
questions.solution: Reports indicated the India–UK CETA would take effect from July 15, 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the currency of Japan?
questions.solution: The Yen is the currency of Japan; Yuan is Chinese, Won is Korean.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the currency of the United Kingdom?
questions.solution: The UK's currency is the Pound Sterling (£).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Where are the headquarters of the United Nations located?
questions.solution: The UN headquarters is located in New York City, USA.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Where is the headquarters of the World Health Organization (WHO)?
questions.solution: The WHO is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is India's national space agency?
questions.solution: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) is India's space agency.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Taj Mahal is located in which city?
questions.solution: The Taj Mahal is in Agra, Uttar Pradesh, on the bank of the Yamuna.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Sun Temple at Konark is located in which state?
questions.solution: The Konark Sun Temple is in Odisha, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In which year did the Dandi (Salt) March take place?
questions.solution: Gandhi led the Dandi Salt March in 1930 during the Civil Disobedience Movement.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Quit India Movement was launched in which year?
questions.solution: The Quit India Movement began in August 1942.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who founded the Maurya Empire?
questions.solution: Chandragupta Maurya founded the Maurya Empire around 321 BCE.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: How many players are there in a cricket team on the field?
questions.solution: A cricket team fields eleven players.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Thomas Cup is associated with which sport?
questions.solution: The Thomas Cup is the premier men's international team badminton event.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Bharat Ratna is India's highest:
questions.solution: The Bharat Ratna is the highest civilian award of India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which institution regulates monetary policy in India?
questions.solution: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is the country's central bank and monetary authority.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: GST in India was implemented from which date?
questions.solution: The Goods and Services Tax came into force on 1 July 2017.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A vessel holds 80 L of milk. 10 L is drawn out and replaced with water; this is repeated 3 times. The milk remaining is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each draw-and-replace keeps the fraction $(1-x/V)$ of milk; after n repeats this fraction is raised to the n-th power.
**Formula.** $M=V\left(1-\tfrac{x}{V}\right)^{n}$
**Given.** V = 80 L, x = 10 L, n = 3.
**Working.** $=80\left(1-\tfrac{10}{80}\right)^{3}$.
**Answer.** $M\approx 53.59$ L.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A invests Rs 5000 for 12 months and B invests Rs 8000 for 10 months. Out of a total profit of Rs 4500, A's share is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit is split in the ratio of capital × time invested by each partner.
**Formula.** $\text{share}_A=P\cdot\tfrac{x\,m_A}{x\,m_A+y\,m_B}$
**Given.** A: Rs 5000×12 mo; B: Rs 8000×10 mo; profit Rs 4500.
**Working.** Ratio $=60000:80000$; share$_A=4500\times\tfrac{60000}{140000}$.
**Answer.** A's share $=$ Rs 1929.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Two trains 140 m and 200 m long move in opposite directions at 54 km/h and 48 km/h. The time to cross each other is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Opposite directions add speeds; convert km/h to m/s with the factor 5/18, then divide the combined length by the relative speed.
**Formula.** $t=\tfrac{L_1+L_2}{(s_1+s_2)\times\frac{5}{18}}$
**Given.** L₁ = 140 m, L₂ = 200 m, s₁ = 54 km/h, s₂ = 48 km/h.
**Working.** Relative $=(54+48)\times\tfrac{5}{18}=28.33$ m/s; $t=\tfrac{140+200}{28.33}$.
**Answer.** $t=12$ s.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A boat covers 140 km downstream in 10 h and the same 140 km upstream in 14 h. Its speed in still water is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Downstream speed adds the stream; upstream subtracts it. Still-water speed is the average of the two.
**Formula.** $u=\tfrac12\left[(b+s)+(b-s)\right]$
**Given.** Downstream 140 km in 10 h; upstream 140 km in 14 h.
**Working.** Downstream $=\tfrac{140}{10}=14$ km/h, upstream $=\tfrac{140}{14}=10$ km/h.
**Answer.** Still-water speed $u=\tfrac{14+10}{2}=12$ km/h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The difference between compound and simple interest on Rs 5000 at 5% per annum for 2 years is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Over 2 years the gap between compound and simple interest is the first year's interest-on-interest.
**Formula.** $CI-SI=P\left(\tfrac{r}{100}\right)^2$
**Given.** P = Rs 5000, r = 5% p.a., t = 2 yr.
**Working.** $=5000\left(\tfrac{5}{100}\right)^2$.
**Answer.** $CI-SI=$ Rs 12.5.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at 5:20 is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The hour hand sweeps 30° per hour and 0.5° per minute, the minute hand 6° per minute; take the smaller of the two angles.
**Formula.** $\theta=\left|30H-5.5M\right|$
**Given.** H = 5, M = 20.
**Working.** $=\left|30\times5-5.5\times20\right|=40^\circ$; smaller $=\min(40,360-40)$.
**Answer.** $\theta=40^\circ$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A trader marks goods 60% above cost, then gives a 20% discount. His net profit percentage is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Net selling factor is the markup factor times the discount factor; profit% is that factor reduced by 1.
**Formula.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{m}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{d}{100}\right)$
**Given.** Markup m = 60%, discount d = 20%.
**Working.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{60}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{20}{100}\right)=1.28$.
**Answer.** Profit $=(f-1)\times100=28\%$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A pipe fills a tank in 6 h, but with a leak it takes 8 h. The leak alone can empty the full tank in:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The leak's rate is the fill-rate without the leak minus the slower fill-rate with the leak.
**Formula.** $T=\tfrac{ab}{b-a}$
**Given.** Fill without leak a = 6 h; with leak b = 8 h.
**Working.** Leak rate $=\tfrac1{6}-\tfrac1{8}$; emptying time $=\tfrac{6\times8}{8-6}$.
**Answer.** $T=24$ h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A vessel holds 50 L of milk. 10 L is drawn out and replaced with water; this is repeated 3 times. The milk remaining is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each draw-and-replace keeps the fraction $(1-x/V)$ of milk; after n repeats this fraction is raised to the n-th power.
**Formula.** $M=V\left(1-\tfrac{x}{V}\right)^{n}$
**Given.** V = 50 L, x = 10 L, n = 3.
**Working.** $=50\left(1-\tfrac{10}{50}\right)^{3}$.
**Answer.** $M\approx 25.6$ L.

questions.stem: What is 35% of 480?

questions.stem: A can do a work in 10 days and B in 24 days. Working together they finish it in:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A and B invest in the ratio 3 : 7. Out of a total profit of Rs 10000, A's share is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The square root of 4225 is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: An article sold at Rs 375 incurs a 25% loss. Its cost price is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: What is the average of the first 16 natural numbers?
questions.solution: Average of first n naturals = (n+1)/2 = (16+1)/2 = 8.5.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The average age of 6 persons is 30 years. If a person aged 40 leaves, the average of the remaining 5 is:
questions.solution: Total = 180; remaining = 180 − 40 = 140; average = 28.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 15 men complete a work in 12 days, 6 men will complete it in:
questions.solution: Men-days constant: 15×12 = 6×D, D = 30 days.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Simple interest on Rs 8000 at 10% per annum for 2 years is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Compound interest on Rs 12000 at 8% per annum for 2 years is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A number divided by 7 gives quotient 12 and remainder 5. The number is:
questions.solution: Number = divisor × quotient + remainder = 7×12+5 = 89.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 30% of a number is 90, the number is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 88 is divided in the ratio 3 : 5, the larger part is:
questions.solution: Larger part = 88 × 5/8 = 55.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A sum doubles itself in 12.5 years under simple interest. The annual rate is:
questions.solution: To double, SI = P, so rate = 100/T = 100/12.5 = 8%.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A 150 m long train crosses a pole in 12 seconds. Its speed is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 6 km towards north, then turns east and walks 8 km. How far is he from the starting point?
questions.solution: Straight-line distance = √(6² + 8²) = √100 = 10 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a certain code, each letter of a word is moved two steps forward in the English alphabet. The code for 'FISH' is:
questions.solution: Replace every letter by the one two places ahead: FISH → HKUJ.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A = 1, B = 2, … , Z = 26, what is the sum of the letter values of 'FAD'?
questions.solution: Add the positional values of F, A, D to get 11.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: X is the husband of Y. Z is the mother of Y. How is Z related to X?
questions.solution: Tracing each relationship step by step gives: Mother-in-law.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term in the series: 2, 5, 11, 23, 47, ?
questions.solution: Each term = previous × 2 + 1, so the next term = 47 × 2 + 1 = 95.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: All pens are books. All books are tables. Which conclusion necessarily follows?
questions.solution: Applying the rules of categorical syllogism, the valid conclusion is: All pens are tables.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a certain code, each letter of a word is moved two steps forward in the English alphabet. The code for 'PEN' is:
questions.solution: Replace every letter by the one two places ahead: PEN → RGP.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A = 1, B = 2, … , Z = 26, what is the sum of the letter values of 'ICE'?
questions.solution: Add the positional values of I, C, E to get 17.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.solution: The only daughter of her mother is the woman herself, so she is the man's mother.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If each letter is replaced by the next letter, FISH is coded as:
questions.solution: Shift each letter forward by one to get GJTI.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If 1 January is a Thursday, what day is 8 January?
questions.solution: 8 − 1 = 7 days, exactly one week, so it is again Thursday.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 9, 16, 25, 36, ?
questions.solution: These are perfect squares; next = 7² = 49.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 9 km north, then 12 km east. His distance from the start is:
questions.solution: Resultant = √(9² + 12²) = 15 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: Z, X, V, T, ?
questions.solution: Letters decrease by 2 each time, so the next letter is R.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: DE, GH, JK, ?
questions.solution: Each pair skips one letter; after JK comes MN.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which of these fractions is the largest?
questions.solution: Comparing decimal values, 4/5 is the largest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A=1, B=2, ... then the sum of the letters of DAB is:
questions.solution: Add the alphabet positions of D, A, B to get 7.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A person facing west turns 90° to the left. Now they face:
questions.solution: Turning left from west leads to South.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: The angle between the hands of a clock at 3:00 is:
questions.solution: Each hour = 30°; at 3:00 the hands are 3 × 30 = 90° apart.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 2, 7, 13, 15?
questions.solution: 2, 7, 13 are prime; 15 is composite.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: All cats are animals. All animals breathe. Which conclusion follows?
questions.solution: Both premises are universal affirmatives, so the chained conclusion 'All cats breathe' follows.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Head : Hat :: Foot : ?
questions.solution: Just as Hat relates to Head, Shoe relates to Foot.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: Cow, Goat, Tiger, Sheep?
questions.solution: Tiger belongs to a different category from the rest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a row of 30 people, X is 5th from the left. X's position from the right is:
questions.solution: Position from right = 30 − 5 + 1 = 26.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 1, 8, 20, 27?
questions.solution: The others are perfect cubes; 20 is not.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'a piece of cake' means:
questions.solution: 'A piece of cake' means something very easy to do.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'once in a blue moon' means:
questions.solution: 'Once in a blue moon' means something happening very rarely.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'to bury the hatchet' means:
questions.solution: 'Bury the hatchet' means to end a quarrel and make peace.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'to let the cat out of the bag' means:
questions.solution: It means to accidentally reveal a secret.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The plural of 'Child' is:
questions.solution: 'Child' has the irregular plural 'children'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The plural of 'Mouse' is:
questions.solution: 'Mouse' has the irregular plural 'mice'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Neither he nor I ___ wrong.'
questions.solution: With 'neither...nor', the verb agrees with the nearer subject 'I', so 'am'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Each of the students ___ a book.'
questions.solution: 'Each' is singular, so it takes 'has'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He is ___ honest man.'
questions.solution: 'Honest' starts with a vowel sound, so 'an' is used.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'She plays ___ violin beautifully.'
questions.solution: Musical instruments take the definite article 'the'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Passive voice of 'John wrote the letter.' is:
options: The letter is written by John
The letter was written by John
questions.solution: Past simple active becomes 'was written' in the passive.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Passive voice of 'She is reading a book.' is:
options: A book is being read by her
A book was read by her
A book has been read by her
A book is read by her
questions.solution: Present continuous active becomes 'is being read' in the passive.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Spot the error: 'He / don't / like / coffee.'
questions.solution: Third-person singular needs 'doesn't', not 'don't'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Spot the error: 'One of the boys / are / playing / outside.'
questions.solution: 'One of the boys' is singular, so it requires 'is'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correct form: 'I ___ my homework already.'
questions.solution: 'Already' signals present perfect, so 'have done' is correct.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correct form: 'By next year, she ___ here for a decade.'
questions.solution: A completed action by a future point uses the future perfect 'will have worked'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the word that means the opposite of 'Artificial'.
questions.solution: 'Artificial' is opposite to 'natural'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correct meaning of the word 'Frugal'.
questions.solution: 'Frugal' means careful with money, i.e. economical.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Abundant'.
questions.solution: 'Abundant' means existing in large quantity, i.e. plentiful.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Brief'.
questions.solution: 'Brief' means short or concise.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Happy'.
questions.solution: 'Happy' is synonymous with 'joyful'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Enormous'.
questions.solution: 'Enormous' means very large, i.e. huge.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Diligent'.
questions.solution: 'Diligent' describes someone hard-working and careful.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Candid'.
questions.solution: 'Candid' means open and frank in speech.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Vivid'.
questions.solution: 'Vivid' means bright, clear and intense.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Routh–Hurwitz criterion is used to determine:
options: The number of closed-loop poles in the right half-plane
questions.solution: Sign changes in the first column of the Routh array count the RHP poles (unstable roots).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A current transformer (CT) secondary should never be:
questions.solution: An open CT secondary produces a dangerously high voltage and core saturation; it must be shorted if the meter is removed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series circuit has L = 0.1 H and C = 40 µF. Its resonant frequency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about ideal sources is INCORRECT?
options: An ideal voltage source maintains its voltage for any load current.
An ideal voltage source has zero internal resistance.
An ideal current source has infinite internal resistance.
An ideal current source has zero internal resistance.
questions.solution: An ideal current source has infinite internal resistance; zero internal resistance describes an ideal voltage source.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a divider, 12 V is applied across 2 Ω and 6 Ω in series. The voltage across the 6 Ω resistor is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The resonant frequency of a series RLC circuit is given by:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, giving f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The type of a control system is the number of:
options: Poles at the origin of the open-loop transfer function
questions.solution: System 'type' = number of integrators (s-factors) in the denominator at s = 0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Total power in a balanced 3-phase load is:
questions.solution: P = √3·VL·IL·cos φ in terms of line quantities.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the divider shown (24 V applied), the output voltage Vout across R2 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transformers are rated in kVA rather than kW because:
options: Their losses depend on voltage and current, not on the load power factor
questions.solution: Iron loss depends on voltage and copper loss on current; both are independent of the load power factor, so apparent power (kVA) is the rating.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a purely inductive AC circuit, the current:
options: Is in phase with the voltage
questions.solution: An ideal inductor makes current lag voltage by 90°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The capacitive reactance of a 50 µF capacitor at 60 Hz is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a balanced star connection, the line voltage is related to the phase voltage by:
questions.solution: In star, line voltage leads phase voltage and VL = √3·Vph; line and phase currents are equal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A sinusoidal voltage has a peak value of 325 V. Its RMS value is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Adding a pole to the open-loop transfer function generally:
options: Reduces stability and makes the response more oscillatory
questions.solution: An extra pole adds phase lag, pushing the root locus toward the right half-plane and reducing stability margins.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The synchronous speed of an AC machine with P poles at frequency f is:
questions.solution: Ns = 120f/P rpm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the series RLC circuit shown (L = 0.2 H, C = 100 µF), the resonant frequency is about:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, so f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)) = 1/(2π√(0.2×100µF)) ≈ 35.6 Hz.

figure.caption: Series RLC circuit

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Power-factor improvement of an inductive load is usually done by connecting:
questions.solution: A shunt capacitor supplies leading reactive power, raising the lagging power factor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the two-wattmeter method the readings are W₁ = 1200 W and W₂ = 300 W. The total 3-phase power is:
questions.solution: Total power = W₁ + W₂ = 1200 + 300 = 1500 W (the difference relates to reactive power).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3-phase synchronous motor is:
questions.solution: It produces no average starting torque and needs auxiliary (e.g. damper-winding) starting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The form factor of a sinusoidal waveform is:
questions.solution: Form factor = RMS/average (half cycle) = 0.707/0.637 ≈ 1.11.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A copper wire (ρ = 1.7×10⁻⁸ Ω·m) is 100 m long with 1 mm² cross-section. Its resistance is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The average value of a sinusoidal current over one complete cycle is:
questions.solution: Over a full cycle the positive and negative halves cancel, giving zero average.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the series RLC circuit shown (L = 0.2 H, C = 10 µF), the resonant frequency is about:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, so f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)) = 1/(2π√(0.2×10µF)) ≈ 112.5 Hz.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" width="300" height="90"><line x1="10" y1="45" x2="40" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="40" y="37" width="34" height="16" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="57" y="32" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">R</text><path d="M74 45 q8 -12 16 0 q8 -12 16 0 q8 -12 16 0" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="98" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">L=0.2H</text><line x1="122" y1="45" x2="150" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="150" y1="35" x2="150" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="158" y1="35" x2="158" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="154" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">C=10µF</text><line x1="158" y1="45" x2="290" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="290" y1="45" x2="290" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="45" x2="10" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="75" x2="290" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="150" cy="75" r="3" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="88" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">~ AC source</text></svg>
figure.caption: Series RLC circuit

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Increasing the air gap in a magnetic circuit (other things equal):
questions.solution: The air gap adds large reluctance (low permeability), so for the same MMF the flux falls.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Thevenin's theorem replaces a linear network by:
options: A current source in parallel with a resistance
A voltage source in series with a resistance
questions.solution: Thevenin: an equivalent EMF Vth in series with Rth seen from the terminals.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A source has Vth = 40 V and Rth = 2 Ω. The maximum power deliverable to a matched load is:
questions.solution: Pmax = Vth²/(4Rth) = 40²/(4×2) = 200 W (load = Rth, efficiency 50%).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A diode conducts when it is:
questions.solution: A diode conducts in forward bias and blocks in reverse bias (ideally).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The sinusoidal voltage shown has a peak of 100 V (marked). Its RMS value is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 120" width="300" height="120"><line x1="10" y1="60" x2="290" y2="60" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="10" x2="20" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M20 60 Q 55 0 90 60 T 160 60 T 230 60 T 300 60" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="55" y1="60" x2="55" y2="10" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="60" y="14" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Vm=100V</text><text x="270" y="74" font-size="10" fill="#475569">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: Sinusoidal voltage waveform

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electrical power is transmitted at high voltage mainly to:
questions.solution: For a given power, higher voltage means lower current, so I²R losses fall.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a balanced delta connection, the line current is related to the phase current by:
questions.solution: In delta, line and phase voltages are equal and IL = √3·Iph.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An induction motor has air-gap power 30 kW and slip 0.05. The rotor copper loss is:
questions.solution: Rotor copper loss = s × Pag = 0.05 × 30 kW = 1500 W.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two identical resistors are connected first in series and then in parallel across the same battery. Which is correct?
options: The parallel combination dissipates four times the power of the series combination.
The series combination dissipates more power.
questions.solution: Series R_eq = 2R (P = V²/2R); parallel R_eq = R/2 (P = 2V²/R) — four times greater for the same voltage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A balanced 3-phase load draws 25 A at 415 V line, pf 0.9. The total power is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Power factor is defined as the cosine of the angle between:
questions.solution: Power factor = cos φ, where φ is the phase angle between voltage and current.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An over-excited synchronous motor draws a current that is:
questions.solution: Over-excitation makes it draw leading current — used for power-factor correction (synchronous condenser).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 48 V source drives a 6 Ω resistor. The current is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a purely capacitive AC circuit, the current:
options: Is in phase with the voltage
questions.solution: An ideal capacitor makes current lead voltage by 90°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a load with R = 3 Ω and reactance X = 12 Ω, the power factor is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At series resonance, the impedance of an RLC circuit is:
questions.solution: XL and XC cancel, leaving only R, so impedance is minimum and resistive (current maximum).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The efficiency of a transformer is maximum when:
questions.solution: Maximum efficiency occurs at the load where variable copper loss = constant iron loss.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in a 470 µF capacitor charged to 10 V is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A step-up transformer has:
options: More secondary turns than primary turns
questions.solution: Step-up means N₂ > N₁, so V₂ > V₁.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A source has Vth = 24 V and Rth = 2 Ω. The maximum power deliverable to a matched load is:
questions.solution: Pmax = Vth²/(4Rth) = 24²/(4×2) = 72 W (load = Rth, efficiency 50%).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Corona discharge on a transmission line increases with:
questions.solution: Corona worsens as surface gradient rises (higher voltage, thinner or rough conductors, bad weather).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As the slip of an induction motor increases beyond the value at maximum torque, the developed torque:
questions.solution: Torque rises with slip up to the breakdown point, then falls as slip increases further (the unstable region).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 4-pole, 50 Hz induction motor runs at 1470 rpm. Its slip is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A moving-iron instrument can measure:
questions.solution: Deflection depends on the square of current, so it works on both AC and DC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the series RLC circuit shown (L = 0.1 H, C = 100 µF), the resonant frequency is about:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, so f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)) = 1/(2π√(0.1×100µF)) ≈ 50.3 Hz.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" width="300" height="90"><line x1="10" y1="45" x2="40" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="40" y="37" width="34" height="16" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="57" y="32" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">R</text><path d="M74 45 q8 -12 16 0 q8 -12 16 0 q8 -12 16 0" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="98" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">L=0.1H</text><line x1="122" y1="45" x2="150" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="150" y1="35" x2="150" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="158" y1="35" x2="158" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="154" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">C=100µF</text><line x1="158" y1="45" x2="290" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="290" y1="45" x2="290" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="45" x2="10" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="75" x2="290" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="150" cy="75" r="3" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="88" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">~ AC source</text></svg>
figure.caption: Series RLC circuit

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tellegen's theorem is based fundamentally on:
questions.solution: Tellegen's theorem holds for any lumped network satisfying KVL/KCL, regardless of element nature.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: When a capacitor is added to correct the power factor of an inductive load, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: The real power (kW) drawn by the load increases.
The reactive power drawn from the supply decreases.
questions.solution: Power-factor correction leaves the load's real power unchanged; it only reduces reactive power, current and kVA.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The impedance triangle shown has R = 3 Ω and X = 3 Ω. The impedance phase angle is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 140" width="200" height="140"><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="180" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="20" y2="10" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="120" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="90" y="134" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=3Ω</text><line x1="140" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="145" y="85" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">X=3Ω</text><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="70" y="78" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Z</text></svg>
figure.caption: Impedance triangle (R–X)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Under maximum power transfer conditions, the efficiency of power transfer is:
questions.solution: With RL = Rth, equal power is lost in Rth and delivered to RL, so efficiency is only 50%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A DC motor on 240 V draws 15 A with armature resistance 0.8 Ω. The back EMF is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Norton's theorem replaces a linear network by:
options: A voltage source in series with a resistance
A current source in parallel with a resistance
questions.solution: Norton: a current source IN in parallel with RN (= Rth).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PMMC (permanent-magnet moving-coil) instrument can directly measure:
questions.solution: PMMC responds to average value; on AC the average is zero, so it reads DC directly (needs a rectifier for AC).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transformer with 500 primary and 400 secondary turns is fed 230 V. The secondary voltage is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transformer operates on the principle of:
questions.solution: Changing flux from the primary links the secondary, inducing an EMF (mutual induction).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 20 Ω, Q = 200 Ω and S = 30 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 20×30/200 = 3 Ω.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 160" width="200" height="160"><polygon points="100,20 180,80 100,140 20,80" fill="none" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="55" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">P=20Ω</text><text x="140" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Q=200Ω</text><text x="55" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=?</text><text x="135" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">S=30Ω</text><circle cx="100" cy="80" r="9" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="100" y="84" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">G</text></svg>
figure.caption: Wheatstone bridge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The V-curve of a synchronous motor is a plot of:
options: Armature current versus field current at constant load
questions.solution: Varying field excitation at fixed load traces a V-shaped armature-current curve, minimum at unity pf.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An induction motor develops maximum torque when the rotor resistance per phase equals:
options: The standstill rotor reactance times the slip (R₂ = sX₂)
questions.solution: Maximum torque occurs at the slip where R₂ = sX₂; the peak torque itself is independent of R₂.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A balanced 3-phase load draws 20 A at 415 V line, pf 0.8. The total power is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A thyristor (SCR) is turned ON by:
options: A gate pulse while forward biased
questions.solution: A gate current triggers the SCR into conduction when the anode is positive; it then latches.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The current-versus-frequency curve of a series RLC circuit peaks at the marked frequency f₀ ≈ 50 Hz. At this resonant frequency the power factor is:
questions.solution: At series resonance XL = XC, the impedance is purely resistive and current peaks, so the power factor is unity. (Here f₀ ≈ 50 Hz.)

figure.caption: Series-resonance current curve

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an induction motor, the rotor copper loss equals:
questions.solution: Of the air-gap power Pag, a fraction s is dissipated as rotor copper loss and (1 − s) becomes mechanical power.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A DC series motor should never be started:
questions.solution: On no load a series motor races to dangerously high speed because flux falls with the small current.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The impedance triangle shown has R = 4 Ω and X = 8 Ω. The impedance phase angle is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 140" width="200" height="140"><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="180" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="20" y2="10" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="120" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="90" y="134" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=4Ω</text><line x1="140" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="145" y="85" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">X=8Ω</text><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="70" y="78" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Z</text></svg>
figure.caption: Impedance triangle (R–X)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The slip of an induction motor running at synchronous speed would be:
questions.solution: Slip s = (Ns − N)/Ns; at N = Ns, s = 0 (and torque would be zero).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A current of 5 A flows through a 12 Ω resistor. The power dissipated is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The commutator in a DC machine acts as a:
questions.solution: It mechanically reverses armature connections so the external current/EMF stays unidirectional.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The superposition theorem is applicable only to:
questions.solution: Superposition holds for linear, bilateral networks; responses of independent sources add.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A sinusoidal voltage has a peak value of 100 V. Its RMS value is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For an ideal buck (step-down) DC–DC converter in continuous conduction, the output voltage is:
questions.solution: A buck converter gives Vo = D·Vs; the boost converter gives Vs/(1−D).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The inductive reactance of a 0.02 H inductor at 60 Hz is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a reciprocal two-port described by ABCD parameters, the condition satisfied is:
questions.solution: Reciprocity of a passive two-port gives the determinant AD − BC = 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Regarding a synchronous motor, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: It runs at exactly synchronous speed in steady state.
Over-excitation makes it draw leading current.
It can be used to improve system power factor.
It is inherently self-starting from rest.
questions.solution: A synchronous motor produces no net starting torque at standstill, so it is not self-starting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The synchronous speed of a 8-pole machine on a 60 Hz supply is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The back EMF of a DC motor is:
questions.solution: Eb = (PφZN)/(60A); it rises with speed and flux and opposes the supply.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 100 Ω, Q = 200 Ω and S = 60 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 100×60/200 = 30 Ω.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 160" width="200" height="160"><polygon points="100,20 180,80 100,140 20,80" fill="none" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="55" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">P=100Ω</text><text x="140" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Q=200Ω</text><text x="55" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=?</text><text x="135" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">S=60Ω</text><circle cx="100" cy="80" r="9" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="100" y="84" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">G</text></svg>
figure.caption: Wheatstone bridge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Why can a transformer not step up a DC voltage?
options: DC has too high a frequency.
Steady DC produces no changing flux, so no EMF is induced in the secondary.
The core saturates instantly for all DC.
DC cannot flow through copper windings.
questions.solution: Transformer action needs dΦ/dt; constant DC gives zero rate of change and hence zero secondary EMF.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Negative feedback in an amplifier generally:
options: Reduces gain but increases bandwidth and stability
questions.solution: Negative feedback trades gain for stability, lower distortion and wider bandwidth (gain–bandwidth product is roughly constant).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the divider shown (12 V applied), the output voltage Vout across R2 is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 220 150" width="220" height="150"><line x1="30" y1="20" x2="30" y2="50" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="22" y="50" width="16" height="34" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="50" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">R1=4Ω</text><line x1="30" y1="84" x2="30" y2="100" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="22" y="100" width="16" height="34" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="50" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R2=6Ω</text><line x1="30" y1="134" x2="30" y2="145" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="30" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="40" y="96" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Vout</text><text x="35" y="16" font-size="10" fill="#475569">12V</text></svg>
figure.caption: Resistive voltage divider

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For maximum power transfer to a resistive load from a DC source, the load resistance should equal:
questions.solution: Maximum power is transferred when RL = Rth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transformer delivers 20 kW with iron loss 0.2 kW and copper loss 0.4 kW. Its efficiency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3-phase induction motor at standstill has a slip of:
questions.solution: At start N = 0, so s = (Ns − 0)/Ns = 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most widely used semiconductor material for power devices is:
questions.solution: Silicon dominates because of its stable oxide, temperature tolerance and cost.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coil has resistance 3 Ω and reactance 4 Ω. Its impedance magnitude is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Ferranti effect on a long, lightly loaded transmission line means:
questions.solution: Line capacitance draws leading current that raises the receiving-end voltage above the sending end under light load.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A DC motor on 230 V draws 20 A with armature resistance 1 Ω. The back EMF is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If one wattmeter in the two-wattmeter method reads zero, the load power factor is:
questions.solution: W₂ = VLIL cos(30°+φ) = 0 when 30°+φ = 90°, i.e. φ = 60°, pf = cos60° = 0.5.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The EMF equation of a transformer is E =
questions.solution: E = 4.44 f N Φm, from the rate of change of sinusoidal flux.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series RLC circuit has R = 10 Ω, L = 0.1 H, C = 40 µF. Its quality factor Q is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For maximum power transfer to a complex load ZL from a source of impedance Zth, ZL must equal:
questions.solution: With a reactive source, maximum power needs ZL = Zth* so the reactances cancel and resistances match.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series circuit has R = 6 Ω, XL = 20 Ω, XC = 8 Ω across 240 V. The current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a single line-to-ground fault, the three sequence networks are connected in:
questions.solution: An LG fault connects positive, negative and zero sequence networks in series.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The skin effect in an AC conductor causes:
options: Current to crowd toward the surface, raising effective resistance
Current to crowd at the centre
questions.solution: At higher frequency current concentrates near the surface, increasing the effective AC resistance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 6 Ω and 20 Ω are connected in parallel. The equivalent resistance is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which property does an ideal operational amplifier NOT have?
questions.solution: An ideal op-amp has infinite (not zero) open-loop gain, infinite input impedance and zero output impedance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 6 Ω and 2 Ω are connected in series. The equivalent resistance is:
questions.solution: Series adds: 6 + 2 = 8 Ω.
